narratives.Williams: The Williams line is the core brick-wall project. The current reliable story begins with George W. Williams, born in 1843, associated with Mississippi/Louisiana, Catahoula Parish, Natchitoches, Leon/Freestone/Navarro County Texas, and Civil War service. Dan Williams’s register is treated as the leading family compilation. Earlier Williams generations in the imported GEDCOM remain hypotheses until Y-DNA and records support them.
narratives.Lee: The Lee line currently runs through Nancy Adeline “Addie” Lee, daughter of Jacob Walker Lee and Nancy Blakely. A key open question is whether Sarah Williams/Lee in George W. Williams’s childhood household connects to Zachariah Lee and whether that Lee line was related to Jacob Walker Lee.
narratives.Blakely: The Blakely/Blakley line runs through Nancy Blakely, wife of Jacob Walker Lee. The current tree includes John William Blakely and Elizabeth Smith, but the older Blakely parentage requires proof because multiple compiled trees conflict.
narratives.Bangs: The Bangs line is one of the deepest imported lines in the GEDCOM, reaching early New England/English candidates. Treat the deep medieval/early colonial stretch as candidate until source reviewed.
narratives.Miller-Mueller: The Miller/Mueller line includes American Miller ancestors and Germanic Mueller/Dotterer/Goebel-style candidate lines. Several generations go deep into Europe and should be reviewed source-by-source.
narratives.Taylor: The Taylor line is traced through William Odis Taylor and earlier Taylor/Murphy/Morrow-linked families in the imported tree. Review county-level records before promoting distant generations.
narratives.Duck: The Duck line currently traces from Velma Duck through John Franklin Duck and older Tennessee/North Carolina Duck candidates.
narratives.Drake: The Drake line appears through the Dodd/Drake side in the imported tree and currently includes DNA-note-marked candidates. Treat older Drake lines as candidates until evidence is reviewed.

researchTargets.title: George W. Williams parentage and Sarah Williams/Lee identity
researchTargets.summary: Resolve whether Sarah Williams/Lee was George W. Williams’s mother and whether Samuel Williams was grandfather, great-uncle, guardian, or unrelated household head. Do not attach Benjamin Williams unless evidence appears.

researchTargets.title: Y-DNA37 Williams line placement
researchTargets.summary: When FamilyTreeDNA Y-DNA37 results post, compare surname, genetic distance, haplogroup, earliest known ancestor, and Williams DNA Project grouping. Set earliest known paternal ancestor as George W. Williams, not speculative older Williams.

researchTargets.title: MCR 1743 / Addie Lee testimony
researchTargets.summary: Find the full Department of Interior / Dawes / Mississippi Choctaw rejected/refused testimony referenced for Nancy Adeline Lee Tyson Williams. Record the claimed lineage and reason for refusal.

researchTargets.title: Jacob Walker Lee and Zachariah Lee relationship
researchTargets.summary: Seek probate, land, court, Bible, or estate evidence proving whether Jacob Walker Lee and Zachariah Lee were brothers/close kin under Samuel Jefferson Lee or related Lee families.

researchTargets.title: John William Blakely parentage
researchTargets.summary: Test Dionysius Blakely + Fanny Unknown vs alternative John Blakely Sr. parentage using probate, deeds, DNA project evidence, and estate files.

researchTargets.title: Little John Smith / Elizabeth Hanna / Newsom conflict
researchTargets.summary: Separate Elizabeth Hanna/Hannah, Elizabeth Newsom, and possible profile conflations. Confirm mother of Elizabeth Smith “Kow-e-moon-tah.”

researchTargets.title: Nancy Williams Little / Basden lead
